design_decisions.thmsmith_propose_topic: Policy-intervention commutator phase for longitudinal causal effects. Pre-anchor check: closest published anchors are Kennedy's incremental propensity-score interventions, longitudinal/resource-constrained IPSI work, Richardson-Robins natural-value interventions, and Diaz-style longitudinal modified treatment policies. Why non-trivial? The target is not another positivity-free identification formula or EIF/path-derivative calculation: require a finite two-time binary transition-kernel witness in which applying an odds-multiplier IPSI and a natural-value modified treatment policy in opposite orders gives two identified but unequal stochastic-policy estimands, plus a closed-form commutator coefficient whose zero set is exactly the no treatment-confounder-feedback/no effect-modification phase. Why promising? The nonroutine object is a hand-derived operator non-equivalence certificate, not a sensitivity bound: a minimal law showing that two standard policy classes that each preserve support do not compose commutatively under longitudinal feedback. If the delta reduces to standard g-formula identification, EIF calculus, or resource-constraint Lagrange multipliers, pivot or accept field-tier.

proposed_from.topic: Policy-intervention commutator phase for longitudinal causal effects. Pre-anchor check: closest published anchors are Kennedy's incremental propensity-score interventions, longitudinal/resource-constrained IPSI work, Richardson-Robins natural-value interventions, and Diaz-style longitudinal modified treatment policies. Why non-trivial? The target is not another positivity-free identification formula or EIF/path-derivative calculation: require a finite two-time binary transition-kernel witness in which applying an odds-multiplier IPSI and a natural-value modified treatment policy in opposite orders gives two identified but unequal stochastic-policy estimands, plus a closed-form commutator coefficient whose zero set is exactly the no treatment-confounder-feedback/no effect-modification phase. Why promising? The nonroutine object is a hand-derived operator non-equivalence certificate, not a sensitivity bound: a minimal law showing that two standard policy classes that each preserve support do not compose commutatively under longitudinal feedback. If the delta reduces to standard g-formula identification, EIF calculus, or resource-constraint Lagrange multipliers, pivot or accept field-tier.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"The closest in-catalogue materials are eid_compositional_reallocation_effect_v1 and eid_proximal_completeness_v1 angle2. The former collapsed to support-span geometry, and the latter left commutator boundary cases promissory. The present draft exhibits the focal coefficient from observed binary transition kernels and frames the open statement as representation non-invariance under ordered composition.","published_axis":"Kennedy2019JASA and DiazWilliamsHoffmanSchenck2023JASA identify IPSI and LMTP classes separately. VanDerLaanLuedtkeDiaz2014EM and WenMarcusYoung2023SMMR motivate representation sensitivity but do not give a finite ordered-policy obstruction. The epsilon-gap is the open-class difference between standalone functional equivalence and ordered-operator equivalence, witnessed by C_{lambda,eta}(r)."}
seed_list: Construct a two-time binary witness where IPSI-after-LMTP and LMTP-after-IPSI are both identified but unequal, with a closed-form commutator coefficient.
Prove a sharp zero-set theorem for the IPSI-LMTP commutator as exactly the no-feedback or no-effect-modification phase.
Give paired SWIG/g-formula criteria for the two ordered IPSI-LMTP compositions and compare their counterfactual independences.
Define a representation-invariant equivalence relation for stochastic-policy operators or prove no such invariant exists for natural-value composition.
Extend the commutator witness to resource-constrained IPSIs with law-dependent scaling factors.
Derive a second-order small-policy commutator derivative for IPSI and LMTP operators and identify its feedback interaction term.
Classify accidental cancellation varieties where the commutator vanishes although feedback and effect modification are both present.
Construct a minimal-support theorem showing two time points and one feedback covariate are necessary for IPSI-LMTP noncommutation.
Build a corrected ordered-policy reporting rule that records the operator order and prevents representation drift in LMTP analyses.
Compare representative random dynamic interventions with natural-value LMTPs by a finite operator non-equivalence certificate.

seed_details.one_line: Construct a two-time binary witness where IPSI-after-LMTP and LMTP-after-IPSI are both identified but unequal, with a closed-form commutator coefficient.

anchor_paper.author: Kennedy; Diaz, Williams, Hoffman, and Schenck
anchor_paper.claim: IPSI and LMTP identification are known separately, but ordered composition is not compared.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: incremental propensity score interventions; longitudinal modified treatment policies

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample stronger than single-law because the seed asks for a coefficient and open-set nonzero witness.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from the longitudinal g-formula because both policies are stochastic interventions.
seed_details.rebuttal: The g-formula identifies each order but does not imply the natural-value kernel is invariant to the prior policy order.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not Kennedy2019JASA or DiazWilliamsHoffmanSchenck2023JASA because it compares two causal operators on one law rather than identifying one operator class.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: the focal object is the commutator coefficient, not another g-formula.

adversarial_referee.instance: A single witness can look field-tier.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The coefficient and open nonzero set promote beyond a hand-tuned example.

adversarial_referee.instance: LMTP papers may already allow ordered stochastic compositions.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: They identify LMTPs, but the Stage -1.1 scan found no commutator theorem.

adversarial_referee.instance: Binary witness could be an artifact of collapsed cells.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The drafted witness keeps all probabilities interior and all treatment kernels distinct.

seed_details.burned_reason: Angles 0-2 failed D-0.5 review; reviewers found routine extended-g-formula identification, promissory/underdefined ordered-policy means, definitional zero-product phase, and no comparator-facing flagship theorem.

seed_details.one_line: Prove a sharp zero-set theorem for the IPSI-LMTP commutator as exactly the no-feedback or no-effect-modification phase.

anchor_paper.author: Young, Hernan, and Robins; Wen, Marcus, and Young
anchor_paper.claim: Natural-value and representation-sensitive interventions are known, but not their algebraic phase boundary.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: depend on the natural value of treatment; observed treatment process

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary theorem stronger than assumption weakening because it asks for necessity and sufficiency.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just a no-effect null restated in policy notation.
seed_details.rebuttal: The target zero set separates feedback from effect modification and excludes accidental marginal equalities.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not YoungHernanRobins2014EM because it gives a polynomial zero phase for two ordered natural-value policies.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: the new object is the phase coefficient and exact zero set.

adversarial_referee.instance: Necessity may be left to generic algebra.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The proof route requires a polynomial open-set zero argument.

adversarial_referee.instance: No-feedback and no-effect-modification are only sufficient.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The conjecture explicitly includes the necessity direction.

adversarial_referee.instance: Zero set may rely on isolated cancellations.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The statement separates open policy rectangles from isolated cancellation varieties.

seed_details.burned_reason: Angles 0-2 failed D-0.5 review; reviewers found routine extended-g-formula identification, promissory/underdefined ordered-policy means, definitional zero-product phase, and no comparator-facing flagship theorem.

seed_details.one_line: Give paired SWIG/g-formula criteria for the two ordered IPSI-LMTP compositions and compare their counterfactual independences.

anchor_paper.author: Diaz, Williams, Hoffman, and Schenck; Sarvet et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Longitudinal LMTPs and limited-resource IPSIs distinguish natural and assigned treatment values.
anchor_paper.location: DiazWilliamsHoffmanSchenck2023JASA Section 3; Sarvet et al. Section 2
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: natural values from assigned treatment values

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Could be routine unless the two orders require different independences.
seed_details.referee_objection: Sequential exchangeability handles both orders with no new condition.
seed_details.rebuttal: One order draws a natural value after a stochastic shift; the other shifts an assigned-policy probability.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not the Diaz LMTP formula because it contrasts two ordered counterfactual worlds.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS if paired criteria differ; field-tier if they collapse.

adversarial_referee.instance: Identification criteria alone may lack a focal object.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Use the ordered-independence frontier as the focal object.

adversarial_referee.instance: Sarvet et al. may not target LMTP composition.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: It is used only for natural versus assigned values in IPSIs.
The seed asks for paired necessity where possible.

seed_details.burned_reason: Angles 0-2 failed D-0.5 review; reviewers found routine extended-g-formula identification, promissory/underdefined ordered-policy means, definitional zero-product phase, and no comparator-facing flagship theorem.

seed_details.one_line: Define a representation-invariant equivalence relation for stochastic-policy operators or prove no such invariant exists for natural-value composition.

anchor_paper.author: van der Laan, Luedtke, and Diaz; Wen, Marcus, and Young
anchor_paper.claim: One statistical estimand can carry distinct causal interpretations, and representations can matter.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: same statistical estimand; multiple modified-treatment-policy representations

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Bridge seed stronger if it produces a non-existence certificate, weaker if purely definitional.
seed_details.referee_objection: Equivalence relation choice is philosophical, not a theorem.
seed_details.rebuttal: The theorem would show no invariant can preserve natural-value composition without extra semantics.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not representation discussion in WenMarcusYoung2023SMMR because it turns representation sensitivity into an impossibility certificate.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS only with non-existence counterexample; otherwise downgrade.

adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The focal object is the equivalence relation plus counterexample.

adversarial_referee.instance: Representation sensitivity may already be resolved.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: No cited paper compares composition invariance.
Require two distinct downstream compositions on the same observed law.

seed_details.one_line: Extend the commutator witness to resource-constrained IPSIs with law-dependent scaling factors.

anchor_paper.author: Sarvet et al.; Young, Logan, Robins, and Hernan
anchor_paper.claim: Resource-constrained and representative stochastic interventions solve realistic allocation problems.
anchor_paper.location: Sarvet et al. Section 2; YoungLoganRobinsHernan2019JASA Section 2

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Routine extension unless the law-dependent multiplier changes the constraint equation by order.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just a Lagrange multiplier added to the base witness.
seed_details.rebuttal: The multiplier is a functional of the post-policy law, so changing order changes the equation.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not Sarvet et al. because it studies ordered resource multipliers under natural-value composition.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS if both scaling factors are solved explicitly.

adversarial_referee.instance: May be too specialized after the base theorem.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Useful as a second-angle pivot, not chosen for v1.

adversarial_referee.instance: Could also identify constrained PRTE-like targets.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Out of scope for the base ExactID phase.

adversarial_referee.instance: Solved multipliers may be sufficient only.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Need necessity of the resource phase.

seed_details.one_line: Derive a second-order small-policy commutator derivative for IPSI and LMTP operators and identify its feedback interaction term.

anchor_paper.author: Kennedy
anchor_paper.claim: IPSI path derivatives are available for odds-scale stochastic interventions.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Derivative-only seed risks collapsing to EIF calculus.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is standard pathwise differentiation of two maps.
seed_details.rebuttal: The nonzero Lie-bracket term would be the new object, but the finite coefficient is cleaner.

seed_details.one_line: Classify accidental cancellation varieties where the commutator vanishes although feedback and effect modification are both present.

anchor_paper.author: Wen, Marcus, and Young
anchor_paper.claim: Representation-sensitive intervention distributions can mask causal distinctions.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: intervention distributions depending on the observed treatment process

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Stronger as a refinement of the zero-set theorem, weak as a standalone algebraic classification.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just solving a polynomial equation after the main result.
seed_details.rebuttal: True as standalone; use as part of Conjecture 1 scope rather than the chosen seed.

seed_details.one_line: Construct a minimal-support theorem showing two time points and one feedback covariate are necessary for IPSI-LMTP noncommutation.

anchor_paper.author: Young, Hernan, and Robins
anchor_paper.claim: Natural-value interventions require longitudinal counterfactual structure.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: interventions that depend on the natural value of treatment

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Minimality can be strong if it rules out all one-time or no-feedback laws.
seed_details.referee_objection: Minimality is bookkeeping after the witness.
seed_details.rebuttal: It would strengthen the witness but should not be the flagship headline.

seed_details.one_line: Build a corrected ordered-policy reporting rule that records the operator order and prevents representation drift in LMTP analyses.

anchor_paper.author: van der Laan, Luedtke, and Diaz
anchor_paper.claim: Distinct causal questions can share one statistical estimand.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: distinct causal questions can lead to the same statistical estimand

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Correction rule without estimator or theorem is too applied.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is reporting guidance, not identification theory.
seed_details.rebuttal: Use only as a consumer of the commutator theorem.

seed_details.one_line: Compare representative random dynamic interventions with natural-value LMTPs by a finite operator non-equivalence certificate.

anchor_paper.author: Young, Logan, Robins, and Hernan
anchor_paper.claim: Representative random dynamic interventions are realistic stochastic longitudinal policies.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Could be a routine comparator unless it yields a non-equivalence certificate.
seed_details.referee_objection: Representative interventions and LMTPs answer different questions by definition.
seed_details.rebuttal: A certificate would quantify when different questions share or do not share a functional.

proposed_from.literature_map: Kennedy 2019 JASA defines IPSIs as odds-multiplier stochastic interventions with longitudinal identification. Diaz and van der Laan 2012 Biometrics is the stochastic-intervention ancestor for population intervention effects. Haneuse and Rotnitzky 2013 Statistics in Medicine introduces modified treatment policies. Young, Hernan, and Robins 2014 Epidemiologic Methods formalizes interventions depending on natural treatment values. van der Laan, Luedtke, and Diaz 2014 Epidemiologic Methods stresses that distinct causal questions can share one statistical estimand. Young, Logan, Robins, and Hernan 2019 JASA studies representative random dynamic interventions. Diaz, Williams, Hoffman, and Schenck 2023 JASA gives longitudinal modified treatment policies and sequential-regression identification. Wen, Marcus, and Young 2023 SMMR highlights representation-sensitive interventions depending on the observed treatment process. Sarvet et al. 2023 Biometrics develops limited-resource IPSIs and distinguishes natural from assigned treatment values. Hoffman et al. 2024 Epidemiology broadens LMTPs to binary, continuous, time-varying, and complex exposures. The open tension is that IPSIs and natural-value LMTPs are each identified, but no paper gives a finite transition-kernel commutator coefficient or exact zero-phase theorem.
